analysis_description: Merged origin/main for Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4 (days 1–3) and round4work/. Ran Phase 1 pipeline `r4_phase1_counterparty_analysis.py`: merges trades with BBO at trade timestamp, infers aggressor (price>=ask aggr_buy, price<=bid aggr_sell), forward mid = mid(ts+K*100)−mid(ts) for K∈{5,20,100} on the 100-tick grid; spread_regime = tight if TOB spread ≤ day×symbol median else wide; burst = ≥2 symbols same (day,timestamp). Cross-asset fwd columns for VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT and HYDROGEL_PACK in events CSV.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate satisfied in `round4_phase1_complete`. Note: `TradingState.market_trades` is never populated in prosperity4bt test_runner (always {}), so live-strategy counterparty hooks need tape CSV ingestion or an engine change.

bullet_summaries.conclusion: Strongest pattern: aggressive BUYs on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT when Mark 22 or Mark 49 is seller show mean forward extract mid +~1.8 to +2.1 at K=5 with high t-stat; Mark 67 as buyer similar. Per-day means for Mark67/Mark22 extract K=5 stay positive on days 1–3.

bullet_summaries.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (cell means + residuals)
bullet_summaries.conclusion: Residuals vs (buyer,seller,symbol,regime,K) cell means show Mark 01→Mark 22 on VEV_5300 has negative LOO residuals on held-out day 1 at K=5/20; Mark 14→Mark 38 hydro stays positive — structured basket residuals on the 01→22 pipe.
Directed graph is sparse (19 edges); Mark 14 is count hub; Mark 01→Mark 22 dominates voucher basket prints; consecutive A→B→C chains reflect same routing.
Multi-symbol same-timestamp bursts vs random timestamps: extract forward mean at K=20 is higher on bursts (~0.22 vs ~0.03); K=5 similar — burst signal is longer horizon.
Ranked mean fwd_same at K=20 by (buyer,seller) under aggr_buy/aggr_sell flags pairs with worst markouts for passive liquidity (see CSV).

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: After aggressive BUY on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT with Mark 22 as seller, extract mid rises ~+2 ticks by K=5 on average
top_5_tradeable_edges.effect: mean fwd_same ≈ +2.0 at K=5; per-day mean +1.8–+2.3 on days 1–3
top_5_tradeable_edges.n: ~86 prints pooled; validate in sim with worse fills before sizing

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: Same with Mark 49 as passive seller on aggressive extract buys
Mark 67 as buyer on aggressive extract buys — forward extract drift
top_5_tradeable_edges.effect: mean ≈ +2.0 at K=5; all three days

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: Multi-symbol same-timestamp burst → higher extract forward at K=20 vs random time control
Mark 22 as seller on aggressive SELLs on VEV_5300 during bursts — negative short-horizon same-symbol fwd (fade candidate)

negative_or_null_findings: K=5 extract forward after bursts is not meaningfully different from random controls; burst signal mainly at K=20.
Most per-participant × VEV cells have too few prints for stable inference outside extract and 01→22 lanes.
Backtester `TradingState.market_trades` is empty during `Trader.run` — tape CSV is authoritative for Mark conditioning until runner exposes trades.
round4work/round4description.txt TTE: only VEV_5000 example; `r4_t_years.py` uses 5−day at open + R3-style intraday wind (confirm if spec updates).

analysis_description: Phase 2 script `r4_phase2_analysis.py`: (1) named-bot burst window ±5 ticks around Mark01→Mark22 timestamps — VEV_5300 fwd5/fwd20 MR fraction ~0.14 (not a clean fade); (2) microstructure — extract spread quartiles vs |Δmid|5 are flat (`extract_spread_quartile_vs_fwd_vol.csv`); joint gate vs short vol shows only day-3 slightly lower vol when tight; (3) lead–lag signed product Δ×lag: VEV_5200/5300 contemporaneous with extract dominates (`leadlag_best_lag_by_day.csv`); hydro weak negative cross at odd lags; (4) Phase-1 extract aggr_buy×Mark22/49 split by **Sonic joint gate** (`phase1_extract_signal_by_joint_gate.csv`) — **refines** Phase-1: when gate tight, day-1 Mark22 mean +0.75 on n=4 (tiny n); pooled pattern is still buyer-side drift but gate slices are count-starved; (5) rough IV residual Mark01→22 by VEV (`iv_residual_*`); (6) worst markouts tail from Phase-1 CSV (`worst_markouts_k20_tail.csv`); (7) Mark55 rolling flow terciles vs extract fwd5 (`mark55_flow_imbalance_vs_extract_fwd5.csv`) — mixed by day.
used_to_decide: Phase 2 gate in `round4_phase2_complete`. `trader_v1.py` implements orthogonal live microstructure + joint gate (sim); counterparty edges remain tape-only until engine exposes trades.

round4_phase2_complete.interaction_with_phase1: Phase-1 aggressive extract buy + Mark22/49 seller **still positive** in wide-book majority of prints; conditioning on **Sonic joint tight** yields **very small n** (e.g. day-1 Mark22 n=4, mean +0.75) — does **not** refute Phase-1 but shows the tape edge is **not** cleanly gated by 5200+5300≤2 on this slice (often opposite sign / low count). Phase-1 burst K=20 lift **confirmed** orthogonal (micro vol vs burst timing). Mark 01→22 VEV_5300 near-burst **does not** show high mean-revert rate (~14%).

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Contemporaneous co-move: extract tick Δ aligns with VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 tick Δ (same timestamp); strongest cross-moment at lag 0 all three days
Avoid passive liquidity in worst tail: aggr_sell Mark49→Mark22 and Mark22→Mark38 show large negative k20 fwd on printed symbol
Extract-only sim with joint gate + microprice skew — positive PnL vs v0 zero (sanity that micro+gate can be wired live)
Joint tight vs wide: slightly lower short-horizon extract |Δmid| on day 3 when gate tight (regime split)
Rough IV residual at Mark01→Mark22 VEV prints — strike-level skew vs ATM IV (for future smile/MM); file iv_residual_mark01_mark22_by_symbol.csv

phase2_negative: Extract TOB spread quartile vs forward |Δmid|5 is flat — no spread-compression directional vol edge in this binning.
Burst-window (±5 ticks of 01→22) VEV_5300: mean-revert fraction only ~14%; not a standalone fade rule.
Mark55 signed-flow terciles vs extract fwd5 flip sign by day — weak inventory signal without more structure.

analysis_description: Phase 3 script `r4_phase3_joint_gate_r4.py`: replicates R3 `analyze_vev_5200_5300_tight_gate_r3` **inner-join** timestamps for 5200+5300+extract; tight=(s5200≤2)&(s5300≤2); K=20 forward extract mid. Pooled Welch **t≈7.94**, tight mean fwd **+0.32** vs loose **−0.12** (`r4_joint_gate_forward_extract_k20_by_day.csv`). inclineGod panels: **spread–spread** scatter per day (`r4_spread_spread_scatter_day*.png`), **spread vs price** via corr s5200/s5300 vs m_ext and vs extract spread in same CSV. **Counterparty × gate:** `r4_aggr_extract_buy_fwd5_by_gate_seller.csv` (Phase-1 style K=5); `mark01_mark22_fwd5_by_gate_and_symbol.csv` shows **Mark01→Mark22 on VEV_5300 when joint_tight** has **negative** mean fwd5 (tape — basket selling into tight surface drags short-horizon mid). `three_way_pair_gate_symbol_fwd5.csv` for Tier-A tables. Read `round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt` and `ORIGINAL_DISCORD_QUOTES.txt` for Sonic/inclineGod wording alignment.
used_to_decide: Phase 3 gate in `round4_phase3_complete`. Tape: Sonic joint gate **cleans** K=20 **extract mid** forward distribution vs Phase-1/2 without that conditioning (Welch highly significant pooled). **Counterparty interaction:** Mark01→Mark22 **VEV_5300** prints under **tight** gate skew **short** at K=5 (negative mean_fwd5) — orthogonal to Phase-1 aggressive-extract-buy drift (different product/horizon). Sim `trader_v2` did not improve PnL vs v1 (no VEV fills); Tier-A remains **tape-first** until MM crosses touch.

round4_phase3_complete.comparison_to_phase1_2_without_gate: Unconditional mid dynamics mixed in Phase 2; **gated K=20 extract forward** is clearly higher when both 5200 and 5300 spreads ≤2 (R4 days 1–3), matching Sonic’s ‘cleaner signal when tight’ narrative on **extract mid** (still not automatic PnL — STRATEGY caveat). Phase-1 Mark22/49 **aggressive extract buy** K=5 means stay positive **mostly when gate loose** (wide majority of counts); **tight slices are n-starved** for that specific print type — gate **does not amplify** that edge in raw count terms.

top_5_tier_a_edges.edge: Sonic joint tight (5200+5300 TOB ≤2, inner-join timestamps) → higher mean K=20 forward extract mid vs not-tight; pooled Welch p ~2e-15
Spread–spread + spread–price: positive corr(5200 spread, 5300 spread) ~0.18–0.31 by day; corr spreads to extract mid ~0.35–0.48 (inclineGod ‘spreads not only prices’)
top_5_tier_a_edges.evidence: same CSV correlation columns; scatter PNGs in outputs/phase3/

top_5_tier_a_edges.edge: Three-way (Mark01→Mark22, joint_tight, VEV_5300): under tight gate, mean fwd5 on 5300 prints is **negative** (basket flow vs short-horizon mid) — candidate **fade / avoid providing liquidity** on that leg when surface tight
Mark14→Mark38 hydro: tight gate shows different mean fwd5 than loose on some days (see three_way CSV) — hydro duopoly interacts with surface regime
Sim `trader_v2` documents that naive wall-mid MM on 5200/5300 under gate **did not fill** in backtester (PnL equals extract-only v1) — execution layer still missing for Tier-A sim

round4_phase3_complete.does_gate_clean_phase1_extract_buy_signa: Partially: **K=20 extract mid** strongly improves under gate (clear ‘clean’ mid signal). The Phase-1 **aggressive extract buy + Mark22/49** **K=5** effect is **not** uniformly larger when tight — cells are **small** and sometimes lower mean; do not equate mid cleanliness with that print-type edge without more n or a sim that fills.

analysis_description: Post–Phase 3 **execution diagnosis** on R4 sim: (1) `r4_vev_fillability_under_gate.py` → `outputs/phase3/r4_vev_spread_under_joint_gate.csv` — under joint tight, 5200/5300 are **mostly 2-tick** but **5–32%** of joint ticks show **1-tick** on a leg (no interior price for bid+1/ask-1 style passive). (2) `trader_v3`/`v4` copied R3 `_vev_orders` but **still 0 VEV PnL** — passive quotes never cross `order_depths` in the matcher when fair is wall-mid inside spread and taker branch `ask<=fair-edge` rarely fires. (3) `trader_v5` **touch takers** (buy at ask, sell at bid, qty 2) prove the **fill path works** but total worse **≈−49.8k** — falsifies always-on touch churn; next sim work needs **selective** liquidity + tape/Mark gating, not blind two-sided hits.
used_to_decide: Ship **tape-first** Tier-A (joint gate K=20 extract); treat **sim VEV MM** as blocked until matcher economics or one-sided flow logic is redesigned. v5 is a **negative result** worth publishing.

analysis_description: Backtested `trader_v6.py` (Round 4): offline JSON `precomputed/r4_m0122_5300_window_joint_signal.json` keys timestamps where joint Sonic tight intersects ±5 ticks of Mark01→Mark22 prints on VEV_5300; live sim sells at best bid (qty 6) on those ticks plus extract v1-style MM. Compared to extract-only v1 (worse total 7514), v6 is **worse net** (1570) because **VEV_5300 −5945** over 3 days swamps unchanged extract **+7515**; day 2 alone **−3914** on the book-total line. **All** match mode total **2051** vs worse **1570** — gap is extract fill quality, not the 5300 leg totals (per-day 5300 PnL lines match across modes in logs). Conclusion: Phase-3 **negative fwd5** on that print/regime does **not** translate to profitable passive **sell-at-bid** in this matcher; next iteration should test **avoid liquidity / widen** or **crossing** hypotheses, or relax signal density.
used_to_decide: **Do not ship** v6 as production: sim falsifies naive translation of tape **short-horizon mid** edge into **repeated bid-side supply** on 5300 — inventory + adverse fills dominate. Keep extract v1 path; park counterparty 5300 fade until execution model or signal timing changes (e.g. post-print delay, size 1, or only when 5300 spread ≥ 2).

analysis_description: Implemented `trader_v7.py` as **v6 with execution hygiene**: require **VEV_5300 spread ≥ 2** before selling at bid, **500 time-unit cooldown** between fade orders (matches preprocess ±5-tick window scale), **qty 2** instead of 6. Backtest worse **5,766** vs v6 **1,570** and v1 extract-only **7,514** — **VEV_5300 still −1,748** pooled but **day-2 book total −144** vs v6 **−3,914** (churn was the main killer). v7 remains **−1,748** worse vs extract-only on the 5300 leg alone; tape-conditioned fade is **not yet additive** to the joint-gate extract MM under `--match-trades worse`, but throttling validates the diagnosis that v6’s **per-tick** quoting inside wide offline windows was mis-specified for sim.
used_to_decide: Prefer **v1** or **no 5300 overlay** for worse-match shipping until 5300 leg turns ≥0 or signal is retimed to **print-aligned** ticks only. v7 is the best **counterparty-overlay** attempt so far but still **costs ~1.75k** vs extract baseline over 3 days.

analysis_description: Extended `preprocess_r4_m0122_5300_gate_signal.py` to emit **`r4_m0122_5300_print_joint_signal.json`**: timestamps of Mark01→Mark22 VEV_5300 trades where `joint_tight` is true on the **same** price row (32/31/69 prints per day vs hundreds of expanded-window ticks). **`trader_v8`** = v7 execution + print-only set. Backtest **worse total 6,554** vs v7 **5,766** vs v1 **7,514**; **VEV_5300 pooled −960** (was −1,748 in v7) — print alignment **cuts sim fade loss ~45%** but overlay still **−960 vs zero**. Day-2 total **+460** worse (v7 was **−144**). **All** total **7,036** vs v1 **7,996** on extract+5300 book.
used_to_decide: Treat **expanded ±window** precompute as overly pessimistic for Tier-A sim overlays; **print-only** is closer to Phase-3 event semantics. Still **no worse-match ship** vs extract-only v1 until 5300 leg ≥0 or strategy changes (e.g. skip fade day-2, or inventory cap).

analysis_description: **Phase-1 counterparty → sim (joint-gated):** `preprocess_r4_extract_aggr_buy_mark22.py` builds 90 timestamps (37/34/19 per day) where extract trades are **aggressive buy** (price≥ask) with **Mark 22** seller. **`trader_v9`** boosts clip to 18 and +1 fair tilt only when that ts is in the set **and** Sonic joint tight. Offline check: **joint_tight ∩ print** counts **4, 6, 8** ticks/day on tape CSV vs 37/34/19 prints — overlay almost never fires. Backtest **worse 7,514** and **all 7,996** **identical to v1** (per-day extract PnL lines match). Conclusion: **do not stack** Sonic gate on this Phase-1 print type for R4 sim without widening the time condition.
used_to_decide: If we revisit this edge, use **±K-tick window** precompute around those prints (like 5300 script) or **drop joint** for this signal family; v9 as written is a **null sim experiment**.

analysis_description: **Phase-1 print overlay without joint:** `trader_v10` applies clip **17** and +1 fair tilt on every precomputed Mark22 aggressive-buy **print** timestamp; base v1 elsewhere. **Worse total 7,648** vs v1 **7,514** (**−134**); day-2 extract **1,402** vs v1 **1,352** drives most of the gap. **All** total **8,878** vs v1 **7,996** (not primary). Falsifies naive ‘always lean long after tape buy’ under **worse** matching — Phase-1 K=5 fwd is not preserved when we translate to **thicker quotes + tilt** at the same clock grid.
used_to_decide: Keep **v1** for worse-match extract baseline; Mark22 aggr-buy overlay is **harmful** in this parameterization. Next tape→sim tests could use **post-print delay** or **one-sided** (bids only) rather than symmetric MM boost.

analysis_description: `trader_v11`: same `r4_extract_aggr_buy_m22_print.json` as v10 but **only** increases **bid** clip by +1 at print timestamps when joint-on (ask stays 16); **no** +1 fair tilt on tape rows. **Worse total 7,590** vs v1 **7,514** (**+76**) and vs v10 **7,648** (**+58**): bid-only asymmetry recovers v10’s worse shortfall and slightly **beats** the extract-only baseline on adverse matching. Day-1 extract line **4,314** vs v1 **4,264** (+50); day-2 **1,377** vs **1,352** (+25); day-3 unchanged **1,899**. **All** total **8,072** vs v1 **7,996** (+76). First Phase-1 counterparty overlay that is **strictly additive** on worse in this sim family.
used_to_decide: Prefer **v11** over v1/v10 for Mark22-print-conditioned extract MM under `--match-trades worse` until a larger sweep or other counterparty rows are tested; v10’s symmetric tilt+size was the wrong translation of the tape long drift.

analysis_description: **Mark49 extension:** `preprocess_r4_extract_aggr_buy_mark49.py` → 99 aggressive-buy prints with Mark 49 seller (32/35/32 by day). Union with Mark22 prints: **69, 69, 51** timestamps/day; **M22∩M49 = ∅** (no same-timestamp overlap). **`trader_v12`** = v11 logic on that union. **`--match-trades worse` total 7,590** — **identical to v11** (per-day extract lines match). So extra Mark49-only ticks did not change adverse-fill economics in this matcher. **`--match-trades all` total 8,112** vs v11 **8,072** (+40; day-2 extract **2,232** vs **2,192**).
used_to_decide: For **worse** shipping, **v12 adds no value over v11** on this 3-day R4 run; Mark49-only windows may still matter under `all` fills. Consider separate Mark49-only trader if we need isolation.

analysis_description: `trader_v13`: identical to v11 except **bid clip +2** (not +1) on Mark22 aggressive-buy **print** timestamps — joint-on bid **18**/ask **16**, off-joint bid **7**/ask **5**. **`--match-trades worse` total 7,616** vs v11 **7,590** (**+26**) vs v1 **7,514** (**+102**). Day-1 unchanged **4,314**; day-2 **1,402** vs v11 **1,377** (+25); day-3 **1,899** unchanged. **`all` total 8,098** vs v11 **8,072** (+26). So modest **monotonic** improvement from larger bid-only lift without symmetric fair tilt; still need more days to claim robustness.
used_to_decide: Prefer **v13** over v11/v12 for worse-match Mark22-print overlay until +3 is tested or drawdown limits appear; v12 union still redundant on worse.

analysis_description: `trader_v14`: **+3** bid clip on Mark22 aggressive-buy print ticks (vs v13 **+2**). **`--match-trades worse` total 7,616** and per-day extract lines **match v13 exactly** (4,314 / 1,402 / 1,899). **`all` total 8,098** — also identical to v13. Interpretation: effective buy fills on those rows were already **not** increasing when order size went from +1→+2; +3 hits the same binding cap (likely `min(mq_bid, lim−pos)` or matcher depth). **Diminishing returns / plateau** at v13 for this 3-day slice.
used_to_decide: Stop increasing Mark22-print bid clip beyond **+2** for this strategy family unless inventory headroom or quote prices change; **ship v13** as the current best worse-match Mark22 overlay (+102 vs v1).
